rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use assign statements only for implementation-level output bindings with existing output features.
Declare auxiliary variables with explicit types matching the expressions they represent.
Avoid referencing AADL properties as AGREE variables without explicit typed ports or constants.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be syntactically valid within annex agree blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees should reference only visible identifiers from the AADL model.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports directly unless represented as variables.
Implementation contracts should not include guarantee statements, only assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Boolean expressions in AGREE require explicit type declarations for auxiliary variables.
Assignments in implementation scope must target existing output features or declared variables.
Range assumptions should use named constants or visible feature names for clarity.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level guarantees in component type contracts.
Do not reference AADL timing properties directly as AGREE variables.
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts without proper variable representation.
Do not generate unsafe Boolean-to-integer conversions for opaque data types.
